title: Paspalum pedicellare
intro: Paspalum pedicellare, commonly known as P. pedicellare or 'False Paspalum', is a tropical grass native to Central and South America, used as a medicinal plant in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of P. pedicellare include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, P. pedicellare is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a paste, to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Paspalum pedicellare is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Paspalum, which includes various species of grasses. The classification of Paspalum pedicellare is further divided into the tribe Andropogoneae and the subfamily Panicoideae. As a member of the Poaceae family, it is also classified as a monocotyledonous plant. Paspalum pedicellare is native to Central and South America, where it is found in a variety of habitats.
uses: Paspalum pedicellare, also known as signal grass, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
